revision.text: '''上野王子'''は[[九十九王子]]の4番目。現在消滅。大阪市天王寺区上之宮町4にある上之宮台ハイツの入り口軒下に「上宮跡」の石碑があり、ここが跡地とされているが、確証は無い。現在は、大江神社（大阪市天王寺区夕陽丘町5-40）に合祀されている。
'''上野王子'''は[[九十九王子]]の4番目。現在消滅。大阪市天王寺区上之宮町4にある上之宮台ハイツの入り口軒下に「上宮跡」の石碑があり、ここが跡地とされているが、確証は無い。現在は、大江神社（大阪市天王寺区夕陽丘町5-40）に合祀されている。 昔、[[四天王寺]]を守る「四天王寺七社」の上之宮神社として、鎮座していた。近くに上之宮高校がある。

'''上野王子'''（'''うえのおうじ'''）は、[[九十九王子]]の4番目。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]上之宮町4にある上之宮台ハイツの入り口軒下に「上宮跡」の石碑があり、ここが跡地とされているが、確証は無い。現在は、大江神社（大阪市天王寺区夕陽丘町5-40）に合祀されている。 昔、[[四天王寺]]を守る「四天王寺七社」の上之宮[[神社]]として、鎮座していた。近くに上之宮高校がある。

'''上野王子'''（'''うえのおうじ'''）は、[[九十九王子]]の4番目。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]上之宮町4にある上之宮台ハイツの入り口軒下に「上宮跡」の石碑があり、ここが跡地とされているが、確証は無い。現在は、大江神社（大阪市天王寺区夕陽丘町5-40）に合祀されている。 昔、[[四天王寺]]を守る「四天王寺七社」の上之宮[[神社]]として、鎮座していた。近くに上之宮高校がある。
*最寄駅 石碑も大江神社も[[谷町線]][[四天王寺前夕陽ケ丘]]か[[JR]][[天王寺駅]]

'''上野王子'''（うえのおうじ）は、[[九十九王子]]の4番目。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]上之宮町4にある上之宮台ハイツの入り口軒下に「上宮跡」の石碑があり、ここが跡地とされているが、確証は無い。現在は、[[大江神社 (大阪市)|大江神社]]（大阪市天王寺区夕陽丘町5-40）に合祀されている。 昔、[[四天王寺]]を守る「四天王寺七社」の上之宮[[神社]]として、鎮座していた。近くに[[上宮高等学校|上宮高校]]がある。
*最寄駅 **大江神社:[[大阪市営地下鉄谷町線]][[四天王寺前夕陽ヶ丘駅]]か[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[天王寺駅]] **石碑：[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄大阪線|大阪線]][[大阪上本町駅]]

かつては[[四天王寺]]を守る「[[四天王寺七宮|四天王寺七社]]」の上之宮[[神社]]として、鎮座していた。近くに[[上宮高等学校|上宮高校]]がある。 *最寄駅 **大江神社:[[大阪市高速電気軌道谷町線|地下鉄谷町線]][[四天王寺前夕陽ヶ丘駅]]および[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]] **石碑：[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪上本町駅]]

revision.text:
''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. ''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2025年1月}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|上宮之址碑]] '''上野王子'''（うえのおうじ）は、[[九十九王子]]の4番目。現在消滅。
[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]上之宮町4にある上之宮台ハイツの入り口軒下に「上宮跡」の石碑があり、ここが跡地とされているが、確証は無い。現在は、[[大江神社 (大阪市)|大江神社]]（大阪市天王寺区夕陽丘町5-40）に合祀されている。
かつては[[四天王寺]]を守る「[[四天王寺七宮|四天王寺七社]]」の上之宮[[神社]]として、鎮座していた。近くに[[上宮高等学校|上宮高校]]がある。 *最寄駅 **大江神社:[[Osaka Metro谷町線|地下鉄谷町線]][[四天王寺前夕陽ヶ丘駅]]および[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]] **石碑：[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪上本町駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] : [[郡戸王子]] - '''上野王子''' - [[阿倍王子神社|安倍野王子]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うえのおうし}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:四天王寺]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:天王寺区の歴史]] [[Category:天王寺区の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|qq=|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|qq=|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|qq=|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|qq=|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|qq=|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|qq=|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
