{{wikidata link|Q11360400}} [[Category:Buildings in Tennoji-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Tennoji-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shitennō-ji]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitenno-ji]]" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station]] or [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]] * '''Stone Monument''': [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] – [[:en:Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|Osaka Uehommachi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360400]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Oe Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Q11436878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station|1=Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station|1=Ōsaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Q11436878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station|1=Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station|1=Ōsaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

{{wikidata link|Q11360400}} [[Category:Buildings in Tennoji-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tennoji-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shitennō-ji]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Q11436878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station|1=Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station|1=Ōsaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上野王子 (大阪市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=January 2025}} [[File:UENO-oji Monument.JPG|thumb|Stone monument marking the site of Ueno Ōji]] '''Ueno Ōji''' (うえのおうじ) was the fourth shrine in the {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} pilgrimage network. The shrine no longer exists.
A stone monument labeled "Site of the Upper Shrine" (上宮跡) stands under the eaves at the entrance of Uenomiyadai Heights, located in Uenomiyachō 4, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. This is believed to be the shrine's former location, though there is no conclusive evidence. Currently, Ueno Ōji is enshrined within {{ill|Oe Shrine (Osaka)|ja|大江神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe Shrine (Osaka)|1=Q11436878|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436878}} (5-40 Yūhigaokachō, Tennoji-ku, Osaka).
In the past, it was part of the "Seven Shrines of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitenno-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}" (四天王寺七社), serving as Uenomiyachō Shrine to protect Shitenno-ji. Nearby is {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358787}}.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Oe Shrine''': {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|en|Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station|WD=Q83603|lt=Shitennoji-mae Yuhigaoka Station|1=Shitennōji-mae Yūhigaoka Station}} or {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}} * '''Stone Monument''': {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} – {{ill|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|en|Ōsaka Uehommachi Station|WD=Q872600|lt=Osaka Uehommachi Station|1=Ōsaka Uehommachi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} – '''Ueno Ōji''' – {{ill|Abe Ōji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Ōji Shrine|qq=}}
